name: British/u.s. Equivalents
recipeIngredient: French bean -- green or string beans
Red, green pepper or capsicum -- bell pepper
recipeInstructions:
Demerara: a brown sugar, but each grain is kind of separate. If you have a Caribbean store in your neighborhood, their brown sugar is what you want. But you can substitute light brown sugar for it.
Muscovado: very dark brown sugar (almost black), sticks together in one solid lump and has to be prised apart. The rawest (and tastiest) of all the sugars. Really good for dark cakes and especially Christmas puddings.
Brown crystal: very large, variagated brown crystals of sugar, hard and crunchy, usually used in coffee.
Jaggery: barely refined cane sugar. Substitute a "raw" sugar or Mexican piloncillo sugar.
Scampi OR Dublin Bay Prawns: Butterfly shrimps or Pacific prawns
